0.q: The SOS1 gene was originally identified in Arabidopsis as one main component of the SOS (salt overly sensitive) signal transduction pathway and involved in maintaining cellular Na+ homeostasis in plants under salt stress.

RT–qPCR was used to test the expression of nine stress-responsive genes, GhSOS1, GhSOS2, GhNHX1, GhCIPK6, GhBIN2, GhSnRK2.6, GhHDT4D, GhCBF1 and GhPP2C, associated with salt stress and cold stress in control and silenced plants.
The SOS2–SOS3 complex leads to phosphorylation and activation of SOS1, a Na+/H+ antiporter localized in the plasma membrane, eventually removing Na+ from the cytosol.
However, information on the cotton SOS pathway is limited... we isolated and characterized the plasma membrane Na+/H+ antiporter gene GhSOS1 in upland cotton.
GhSOS1, homologous to AtSOS1, was isolated from a salt-tolerant genotype of G. hirsutum. Silencing of GhSOS1 significantly reduced cotton tolerance to salt.

3.q: Expression of GhNHX1 suppressed the alkali cation-sensitive phenotype of the mutant, indicating that GhNHX1 was orthologous to yeast ScNHX1, confirming the vacuolar localization of GhNHX1.
GhNHX1 was proved to be located in the vacuolar system and intensively induced by salt stress in cotton.

Wu et al. (2004) demonstrated that there is a positive correlation between the increased transcript level of GhNHX1 and the more salt-tolerant cultivar, indicating that the cotton vacuolar Na+/H+ antiporter probably plays a major role in salt tolerance in cotton.
yeast strain AXT3 transformed with GhNHX1A and yeast strain AXT3 transformed with GhNHX4A were more tolerant to salt stress, likely because of a partially recovered Na+ transport function

6.q: Silencing of GhABF3 sensitized cotton to drought and salt stress, which was manifested in decreased cellular antioxidant capacity... Overexpressing GhABF3 significantly improved salt tolerance of upland cotton by reducing the degree of cellular oxidation.
Both GhABF3 and GhMPK3 overexpression increases cotton tolerance to salt and drought through improving antioxidant activities.

7.q: Recombinant proteins of MBP-GhRAF36, MBP-GhFER, MBP-GhLYK3, and MBP-GhSRF6 were incubated with a gradient concentration of His-GhTOPP4aD for the dephosphorylation assay.
GhTOPP4aD could also strongly abolish the autophosphorylation of GhRAF36 (Figure 3d), similar to CIPA treatment.

Our ultimate result of hub gene analysis revealed precisely that SOS3/CBL10-SOS2 network plays a core role in specific regulation of salt-alkali stress in upland cotton.

16.q: GhTOPP4aD could significantly inhibit the kinase activity of GhRAF36 under normal conditions; GhRAF36 kinase activity is enhanced in VIGS-GhTOPP4aD and reduced in OE-GhTOPP4aD plants.
GhRAF36, FERONIA (GhFER), and Lysin Motif-containing Receptor-like Kinase 3 (GhLYK3) interacted with GhTOPP4aD and their kinase activities were inhibited by GhTOPP4aD.

17.q: Silencing of GhGLDH35A in cotton resulted in reduced tolerance to alkaline stress, disruption of ROS homeostasis... overexpression of GhGLDH35A in Arabidopsis enhanced alkaline stress resistance by elevating AsA levels, increasing antioxidant enzyme activities to enhance ROS scavenging.

18.q: Overexpression of GhGLDH35A in Arabidopsis enhanced alkaline stress resistance by elevating AsA levels, increasing antioxidant enzyme activities to enhance ROS scavenging, sustaining photosynthesis, and promoting stomatal closure.

19.q: GhPIP2;7-silenced plants decreased in the chlorophyll content, superoxide dismutase (SOD) activity, and peroxidase (POD) activity comparing the mock control under 400 mM NaCl treatment, which indicated a positive regulatory role of GhPIP2;7 in salt tolerance.

20.q: GhTIP2;1-overexpressed plants exhibited less accumulation of H2O2 and malondialdehyde but higher proline content under osmotic stress.

22.q: target gene-silenced plants showed significantly higher chlorophyll degradation, higher H2O2, malondialdehyde (MDA) and proline accumulation but significantly reduced superoxide dismutase (SOD) activity, suggesting their reduced ability to effectively degrade accumulated reactive oxygen species (ROS).

23.q: target gene-silenced plants showed significantly higher H2O2, malondialdehyde (MDA) and proline accumulation but significantly reduced superoxide dismutase (SOD) activity, suggesting their reduced ability to effectively degrade accumulated reactive oxygen species (ROS).
